blocks.title: Creating Arrays

blocks.content:
## Accessing Array Items

Each item has an **index** (position). Indexes start at 0, not 1!

```javascript
let fruits = ['apple', 'banana', 'orange'];
console.log(fruits[0]);  // 'apple' (first item)
console.log(fruits[1]);  // 'banana' (second item)
console.log(fruits[2]);  // 'orange' (third item)
```

blocks.title: Array Indexing

blocks.content:
## Array Length

The `.length` property tells you how many items are in the array.

blocks.title: Array Length and Loop

blocks.instruction: Get the second item from an array named 'items':
blocks.explanation: Array indexes start at 0, so the second item is at index 1.

questions.question: What index is the first item in an array?
questions.explanation: Array indexes start at 0. The first item is at index [0].

questions.question: If an array has 5 items, what is the index of the last item?
questions.explanation: With 5 items, indexes are 0, 1, 2, 3, 4. The last item is at index 4.
